examples.title: Water Quality Index Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate WQI based on multiple water quality parameters
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_WQI
INPUT: DO, BOD, COD, pH, Nitrate, Phosphate, Turbidity
OUTPUT: WQI (Water Quality Index)

BEGIN
 // Define weight for each parameter
 weights = {DO: 0.2, BOD: 0.15, COD: 0.1, pH: 0.15, 
 Nitrate: 0.15, Phosphate: 0.1, Turbidity: 0.15}
 
 // Normalize each parameter (0-100 scale)
 q_DO = normalize_DO(DO) // Higher is better
 q_BOD = normalize_BOD(BOD) // Lower is better
 q_COD = normalize_COD(COD) // Lower is better
 q_pH = normalize_pH(pH) // Optimal around 7
 q_Nitrate = normalize_Nitrate(Nitrate) // Lower is better
 q_Phosphate = normalize_Phosphate(Phosphate) // Lower is better
 q_Turbidity = normalize_Turbidity(Turbidity) // Lower is better
 
 // Calculate sub-indices
 sub_indices = [q_DO, q_BOD, q_COD, q_pH, q_Nitrate, q_Phosphate, q_Turbidity]
 
 // Calculate weighted sum
 WQI = 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO 6 DO
 WQI = WQI + (sub_indices[i] * weights[i])
 END FOR
 
 // Classify water quality
 IF WQI >= 90 THEN
 classification = "Excellent"
 ELSE IF WQI >= 70 THEN
 classification = "Good"
 ELSE IF WQI >= 50 THEN
 classification = "Fair"
 ELSE IF WQI >= 25 THEN
 classification = "Poor"
 ELSE
 classification = "Very Poor"
 END IF
 
 RETURN WQI, classification
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates Water Quality Index by normalizing multiple parameters and applying weights. Each parameter is scored based on how close it is to ideal values, then combined into a single index value indicating overall water quality.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of parameters (7)

examples.title: BOD Calculation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Biochemical Oxygen Demand from dilution series
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_BOD
INPUT: initialDO, finalDO, dilutionFactor, sampleVolume, seedVolume
OUTPUT: BOD (mg/L)

BEGIN
 // Method 1: Direct measurement
 IF dilutionFactor = 1 THEN
 BOD = initialDO - finalDO
 
 // Method 2: Dilution method
 ELSE
 // Calculate total volume
 totalVolume = sampleVolume + seedVolume + dilutionWater
 
 // Apply dilution factor
 DO_consumed = initialDO - finalDO
 
 // Calculate BOD with dilution
 BOD = (DO_consumed * totalVolume) / sampleVolume
 END IF
 
 // Adjust for seed correction if needed
 IF seedCorrectionRequired THEN
 seedBOD = Calculate_Seed_BOD()
 BOD = BOD - (seedBOD * (seedVolume / totalVolume))
 END IF
 
 // Ensure non-negative
 IF BOD < 0 THEN
 BOD = 0
 END IF
 
 RETURN BOD
END

// Helper function for normalization
FUNCTION normalize_BOD(value)
BEGIN
 // Lower BOD is better (unpolluted: <1 mg/L, polluted: >8 mg/L)
 ideal = 0
 worst = 10
 score = 100 * (worst - value) / (worst - ideal)
 RETURN clamp(score, 0, 100)
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates BOD by measuring the difference in dissolved oxygen before and after a 5-day incubation period. The dilution method is used when sample oxygen demand exceeds available oxygen.
